# Ops Workspace

## Infrastructure

Cloudflare Pages hosts both the static frontend (`client/public/`) and the
backend (`client/functions/`, Cloudflare Pages Functions running on the
Workers runtime). Data lives in Cloudflare D1 (SQLite at the edge) — no
server to patch, no process to keep alive, no local disk to back up.

## Folder structure: /test vs /client

* **`/test`** — dev working copy. All hand-edits happen here; `npm run dev` runs from here.
* **`/client`** — production copy, generated by `ops/scripts/sync-to-client.sh`. This is the folder Cloudflare Pages actually builds and deploys — never hand-edit it, the next sync overwrites it.

Promote a verified change: `ops/scripts/sync-to-client.sh` (`--dry-run` to
preview), then commit/push `/client`.

## Deployment

Deploys are push-based and zero-downtime — see `docs/guides/DEPLOYING.md` for
the full explanation of why an update never interrupts live traffic
(mid-campaign or otherwise). In short:

* The Cloudflare Pages project is connected to this repo's `main` branch, root directory `client`. Every push builds and deploys automatically; every branch/PR gets its own preview URL to check before merging.
* Manual deploy (rare — normally CI does this): `cd client && npm run deploy`.
* D1 schema changes are applied separately from the app deploy: `npm run db:migrate:remote`. Migrations must be additive-only (see the deploy guide) so the old and new app versions can both run against the same database during the rollout window.
* Health check: `GET /api/health`.
* Rollback: re-promote a previous deployment from the Cloudflare Pages dashboard — no redeploy needed, no downtime.

## Runbook Conventions

* One runbook per operational task in `scripts/` or as a `RUNBOOK-*.md` here; every runbook starts with "when to use this".
* Before any migration that drops/renames a column, export the affected table first (`wrangler d1 execute legal-connections --remote --command "SELECT * FROM leads"` or use the D1 dashboard export).
* Never commit or share lead data — it contains client PII. D1 database contents are never checked into the repo; `test/migrations/seed_dev_leads.sql` is historical dev-only sample data, not real customer data.
* Secrets (`CONTACT_EMAIL`, `CONTACT_SENDER_EMAIL`, `AWS_SES_KEY`, `AWS_SES_SECRET`, `AWS_REGION`, etc.) are set with `wrangler pages secret put <NAME>` — never in `wrangler.toml` or any committed file. Default `CONTACT_SENDER_EMAIL` if unset: `do-not-reply@legal-connections.com`.
